digest.top_line: MDT/SBRT delays progression across oligomet prostate states; ARTO is the first RCT to suggest an OS benefit.
digest.tldr: Single-site day. Fonteyne's EAU 2026 review maps the oligomet prostate landscape for RT: ARTO leads with an OS signal in castration-resistant disease; WOLVERINE shows PFS/CRPC delay without OS; de novo HSPC awaits STAMPEDE2. Selection, not lesion count, is the actionable gate.

sites.intro: MDT/SBRT evidence is maturing across oligomet prostate states, with ARTO providing the first RCT OS signal, but de novo HSPC and patient selection remain open.

studies.name: EAU 2026: MDT/SBRT in oligometastatic prostate cancer (Fonteyne review)
studies.tldr: MDT/SBRT delays progression across oligomet prostate states; only ARTO suggests an OS benefit; de novo HSPC and patient selection remain unsettled.
details: 🔍 EAU 2026 thematic-session review (Fonteyne, Ghent radonc) of MDT/SBRT evidence across oligomet prostate states, not a single trial
📊 Oligorecurrent HSPC carries most data: STOMP, ORIOLE showed high local control, delayed progression, minimal toxicity (no effect sizes reported in source)
📊 RADIOSA: ADT plus MDT improved PFS vs MDT alone (no effect size reported in source)
📊 WOLVERINE: MDT added to SOC improved PFS, rPFS and delayed CRPC, but no significant OS benefit (no effect sizes reported in source)
🔗 ARTO (castration-resistant oligomet): MDT plus SOC improved PFS, OS and PCSS; per Fonteyne the first RCT signal of an OS benefit (no effect sizes reported in source)
⚠️ De novo oligomet HSPC: high-level evidence lacking; awaiting STAMPEDE2 (SBRT), PLATON, TERPS, OLIGOPRESTO
⚠️ Radioligand plus MDT unproven: RAVENS (Ra-223 plus MDT) did not improve PFS or MFS vs MDT alone; LUNAR (Lu-177) ongoing
🔍 SOLAR (N=21, synchronous): tested ADT discontinuation after MDT, tracking testosterone recovery with sustained low PSA; outcomes favored synchronous vs SATURN (metachronous), hypothesis-generating
⚠️ Selection gates benefit, not lesion count alone: PSMA-PET burden (PP3 retrospective) may refine OS stratification; systemic therapy alone remains standard for most pts

verdict.rationale: Narrative conference review of small, heterogeneous phase 2/3 MDT trials; PFS-dominant endpoints, single RCT (ARTO) hinting OS; guideline integration and selection unresolved.

open_questions: Does MDT improve OS beyond PFS in oligometastatic prostate?
Role of MDT in de novo oligometastatic HSPC

related_trials.brief_title: Comprehensive Versus Primary Tumor Radiotherapy in Oligometastatic Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This study is a multicenter, randomized controlled phase III clinical trial (PROLONG-3) designed to evaluate the survival benefit of comprehensive radiotherapy combined with primary tumor radiotherapy versus primary tumor radiotherapy alone in patients with newly diagnosed oligometastatic prostate cancer. The trial enrolled 390 patients with ≤10 metastatic lesions confirmed by PSMA PET imaging, who were randomized in a 2:1 ratio to either the intervention group (comprehensive radiotherapy +…
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Male patients aged 18-85 years.
* Histopathologically confirmed acinar adenocarcinoma of the prostate. The presence of a minor component of ductal adenocarcinoma, intraductal carcinoma, and/or neuroendocrine differentiation is permitted.
* PSMA PET performed within 4 weeks…
related_trials.answers_question: Does MDT improve OS beyond PFS in oligometastatic prostate?
related_trials.relevance_phrase: phase III; MDRT vs primary RT alone; survival endpoint

related_trials.brief_title: Prostate-cancer Treatment Using Stereotactic Radiotherapy for Oligometastases Ablation in Hormone-sensitive Patients
related_trials.brief_summary:
INDICATION: Oligometastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer patients. METHODOLOGY: Open label, double arm, randomized 1:1, multicenter phase III study.

PRIMARY OBJECTIVE: To assess the efficacy of ablative radiotherapy (SBRT applied to all oligometastases) administered to all gross tumor sites (metastases and prostate if applicable), in oligometastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer patients.

interventions.name: Stereotactic Body Radiotherapy (SBRT) + Standard of care

related_trials.answers_question: Role of MDT in de novo oligometastatic HSPC

related_trials.brief_title: TERPS Trial for de Novo Oligometastic Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This research is being done to see if we can improve the outcome of prostate cancer patients who present with metastatic lesions at initial diagnosis.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. Patient must have at least one and up to three asymptomatic metastatic tumor(s) of the bone or soft tissue (with at least one bone metastasis) develop within the past 6-months that are seen on imaging. Up to five lesions are allowed on advanced functional imaging such as…

related_trials.brief_title: Intermittent Androgen Deprivation Therapy With or Without Stereotactic Body Radiotherapy for Molecularly Identified Hormone Sensitive Oligometastatic Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This study is evaluating whether adding stereotactic radiotherapy (a new, more focused type of radiotherapy) to treat all the tumours that are present will improve outcomes or not compared to drugs alone for patients who are negative on conventional imaging and positive on PSMA PET scan
related_trials.eligibility_brief:

1. Age \> 18 years.
2. Able to provide informed consent.
3. Histologic diagnosis of prostate adenocarcinoma.
4. ECOG performance status 0-1.
5. Stage IV hormone sensitive, synchronous or metachronous oligometastatic prostate cancer as detected by PSMA PET-CT scan with no…
related_trials.relevance_phrase: PSMA-PET only selection; conventional imaging negative

related_trials.brief_title: Phase II Non-Randomized Study Evaluating POSLUMA-PSMA PET Response After Oligo- Metastatic/Progressive-directed Treatment With Radiotherapy (PROMPT-R)
related_trials.brief_summary: This study aims to evaluate the role of PSMA-PET and circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) in monitoring prostate cancer progression and response to radiotherapy (RT). Specifically, it investigates how often early progression is detected on PSMA-PET before PSA rise, examines PET responses of lesions treated with RT at 6 and 12 months and their correlation with PSA changes and later progression, examines ctDNA changes following ablative-intent RT to metastatic sites, and explores the relationship…
related_trials.eligibility_brief:

* Documented informed consent of the participant or legally authorized representative.
* Age: ≥ 18 years
* KPS ≥ 70 or ECOG 0-1 (Appendix A)
* Biopsy-confirmed diagnosis of castration-sensitive prostate adenocarcinoma, as defined by rising PSA in setting of T\>100

related_trials.relevance_phrase: PSMA-PET + ctDNA as response biomarkers post-MDT

papers.title: EAU 2026: What Evidence Do We Have from Intensification with SBRT?
